title: Not Just Heatwave, Kerala Sees UV Radiation Spike: Know How To Protect Yourself - Times Now
full_text:
High levels of ultraviolet radiation have been recorded across Kerala over the past 24 hours, the Kerala State Disaster Management Authority (KSDMA) said on Friday, raising concerns over rising heat stress conditions in the state. As per data shared by the authority, UV levels above eight, which warrant an orange alert, were reported at Konni in Pathanamthitta, Chengannur in Alappuzha, Changanassery in Kottayam and Munnar in Idukki.
Prolonged exposure to UV rays can cause sunburn, skin diseases, eye problems and other health issues, the authority said.
In addition, UV levels between six and seven, corresponding to a yellow alert, were recorded at 10 locations across the state, indicating widespread exposure risk even beyond high-altitude regions. Prolonged exposure to UV rays can cause sunburn, skin diseases, eye problems and other health issues, the authority said.
What regions in Kerala are under heavy rainfall alerts?
What should residents do during high-energy swell waves?
What are the health risks associated with heatwaves?
Peak Hours Identified, Public Urged to Stay Indoors
According to the advisory by Kerala State Disaster Management Authority, peak UV levels are usually recorded between 10 am and 3 pm, and people are urged to avoid direct sunlight exposure during these hours as much as possible.
Those engaged in outdoor work, fishermen involved in marine and inland fishing, water transport workers, bike riders, tourists, and individuals with skin conditions, albinism, eye diseases, cancer and weakened immunity have been asked to exercise extra caution.
The authority advised the public to use protective measures such as hats, umbrellas and sunglasses, and to wear full-body cotton clothing while stepping out during the day.

title: Heatwave alert issued in Kerala as temperature soar - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Thiruvananthapuram,24 April (HS): Kerala is bracing for an intense spell of heat, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) warning of possible heatwave conditions in parts of the State over the coming days.
Orange alerts have been issued for Palakkad, Thrissur, and Kollam districts, where temperatures are expected to rise significantly above normal levels.
According to the IMD, Palakkad may record temperatures as high as 41°C, while Thrissur and Kollam could touch 40°C. Several other districts, including Kottayam, Pathanamthitta, Ernakulam, and Kozhikode, are likely to see temperatures climb up to 38°C. In Alappuzha, Malappuram, Kannur, and Kasaragod, the mercury may hover around 37°C. Even coastal Thiruvananthapuram is expected to experience temperatures up to 36°C-around 4 to 5 degrees above the seasonal average.
Heatwave conditions are considered rare in Kerala, with previous instances recorded in 2016 in Palakkad and Thrissur, and more recently in 2024 in Palakkad and Kozhikode. The IMD has cautioned that temperatures across at least 12 districts will remain 3 to 4 degrees above normal until April 26.
In response to the extreme heat, district administrations in Thrissur and Kollam have declared a holiday for educational institutions. Authorities have also directed that anganwadis, tuition centres, and vacation classes should remain closed. However, online classes may be conducted if necessary. Scheduled examinations by universities and boards will proceed as planned.
Meanwhile, there is some relief in sight. The IMD has forecast the possibility of summer showers beginning April 27, which could bring down temperatures. Rainfall is expected to continue in the following days, offering respite from the prevailing heatwave conditions.
---------------
Hindusthan Samachar / Arun Lakshman

title: Kerala Heatwave Alert: IMD Issues Orange Warning For Palakkad, Thrissur As Temperatures Hit 40 Degrees | Forecast - The Daily Jagran
full_text:
Kerala Weather Update: Kerala continues to reel under an intense spell of heat, prompting the India Meteorological Department (IMD) to issue an orange alert for heatwave conditions across several parts of the state. The weather department has warned that relief is unlikely to come soon, with districts such as Palakkad and Thrissur expected to experience significantly high temperatures on April 25 and 26.
Authorities have advised residents to take precautionary measures and limit outdoor activities during afternoon peak hours to mitigate health risks.
Kerala Weather Forecast
Weather authorities warn that heatwave conditions are set to intensify, with isolated areas in Palakkad district likely to record maximum temperatures of around 41 degrees Celsius. Thrissur is expected to follow closely with a maximum temperature of 40 degrees Celsius.
Several other districts, including Kollam, Kottayam, Pathanamthitta, Ernakulam, and Kozhikode, are likely to witness significantly above-normal levels, ranging between 38 degrees Celsius and 39 degrees Celsius.
Coastal and northern districts, including Alappuzha, Malappuram, Kannur, and Kasaragod, are likely to see maximum temperatures of 37 degrees Celsius, while Thiruvananthapuram is expected to record highs of around 36 degrees Celsius.
Hot And Humid Conditions Expected
In addition to the soaring temperatures, hot and humid conditions are expected to persist across most districts, particularly in non-hilly regions. According to the weather authorities, the combination of heat and humidity is likely to increase physical discomfort and health risks for vulnerable groups, including the elderly, children, and individuals with pre-existing medical conditions.
Advisory For Residents
Residents have been advised to take precautionary steps, such as staying well-hydrated, wearing light, breathable clothing, and avoiding direct exposure to sunlight during peak afternoon hours. To mitigate health risks, authorities have advised residents to limit outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours and stay updated with IMD weather advisories.
